title: Epitaphs
content:
An epitaph in an old Moravian cemetery reads thus:

     Remember, friend, as you pass by,
     As you are now, so once was I;
     As I am now thus you must be,
     So be prepared to follow me.

    There had been written underneath in pencil, presumably by some wag:

     To follow you I'm not content
     Till I find out which way you went.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Epitaphs", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
